hooks.command: echo ''; echo '●─▶●─▶●─▶●  relay'; echo ''; echo 'sprint backlog:'; if ls _work/sprint-*.json >/dev/null 2>&1; then ls _work/sprint-*.json | sort -V | xargs -I{} basename {} .json | sed 's/^/    /'; else echo '    (none — _work/ is empty)'; fi; echo ''; echo 'recent commits:'; git log --oneline -5 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/    /' || echo '    (not a git repo yet — git init when ready)'; echo ''

python3 -c 'import sys, json, os; data=json.load(sys.stdin); fp=data.get("tool_input",{}).get("file_path",""); blocked = ("/_specs/" in fp) or ("/_work/sprint-" in fp and fp.endswith(".json") and os.path.exists(fp)); sys.exit(2 if blocked else 0)' 2>/dev/null && exit 0 || { echo 'BLOCKED: spec files (_specs/) and sprint backlog (_work/sprint-*.json) are frozen sources of truth. Do not edit them — they define what gets built. If a spec is wrong, raise it with the user before changing.' >&2; exit 2; }

python3 -c 'import sys, json, re; data=json.load(sys.stdin); cmd=data.get("tool_input",{}).get("command",""); patterns=[r"\bnpm\s+publish\b", r"\bpnpm\s+publish\b", r"\bgit\s+push\s+(-f|--force)\b", r"\bgit\s+reset\s+--hard\b", r"\brm\s+-rf\s+/(?!tmp|var/folders)", r"\brm\s+-rf\s+\.git\b", r"\brm\s+-rf\s+_specs\b", r"\brm\s+-rf\s+_work\b"]; matched=[p for p in patterns if re.search(p, cmd)]; sys.stderr.write("BLOCKED: command matches a destructive pattern ("+matched[0]+"). If this is intentional, ask the user to run it manually.\n") if matched else None; sys.exit(2 if matched else 0)'

python3 -c 'import sys, json; data=json.load(sys.stdin); cmd=data.get("tool_input",{}).get("command",""); print("  →", "committed" if "git commit" in cmd else "", end="") if "git commit" in cmd else None' 2>/dev/null; if echo "$0" | python3 -c 'import sys, json; data=json.load(sys.stdin); cmd=data.get("tool_input",{}).get("command",""); sys.exit(0 if "git commit" in cmd else 1)' 2>/dev/null; then SHA=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null); SUBJ=$(git log -1 --pretty=%s 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$SHA" ] && echo "  → committed $SHA: $SUBJ"; fi

CHANGED=$( { git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null; git diff --name-only --cached 2>/dev/null; git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null; } | sort -u); PKGS=$(echo "$CHANGED" | grep -E '^(packages/[^/]+|examples/[^/]+)/' | sed -E 's|^((packages|examples)/[^/]+)/.*|\1|' | sort -u); if [ -n "$PKGS" ]; then echo ''; echo 'changed paths this session touched:'; echo "$PKGS" | sed 's/^/    /'; echo ''; echo 'verify with:'; echo "$PKGS" | awk -F/ 'NR<=4 {print "    pnpm -F " $NF " typecheck"}'; echo ''; fi
